active_paradigms.idea: Use scipy.optimize.minimize with trust-constr method optimizing only positions (2n=52 variables), with LP-computed radii in objective
active_paradigms.description: Instead of optimizing 3n variables with SLSQP, optimize only 2n position variables using trust-constr. The objective function computes optimal radii via LP for current positions and returns negative sum. Trust-constr handles the 325 nonlinear non-overlap constraints better than SLSQP. Use corner-weighted initialization: place 4 larger circles at corners, then hexagonal packing for interior. The trust-constr method uses interior-point or trust-region methods better suited for many nonlinear constraints.
active_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Primary: sum of radii. Focus on position optimization quality since LP gives optimal radii for any position configuration.
active_paradigms.cautions: Trust-constr requires constraints as separate objects. Use NonlinearConstraint for pairwise distances. Initialize with positions that satisfy constraints to avoid infeasible starting point. Use verbose=2 to monitor convergence. Set maxiter high enough (2000+) for 52 variables.

active_paradigms.idea: Use scipy.optimize.basinhopping with custom position perturbation and LP-based radius computation for global optimization
active_paradigms.description: Basinhopping combines random perturbation with local minimization to escape local optima. Define state as 2n position variables. The objective computes LP-optimal radii and returns negative sum. Use custom take_step function that perturbs positions while maintaining feasibility (small Gaussian steps). Local minimization uses SLSQP or trust-constr. Run for 500-1000 iterations with temperature parameter tuned for acceptance probability. This stochastic approach can find better global optima than deterministic SLSQP from fixed initializations.
active_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Primary: sum of radii. Secondary: escape local optima through stochastic search.
active_paradigms.cautions: Basinhopping can be slow - set niter appropriately for timeout. The accept_test function should reject infeasible states. Use seed for reproducibility. Temperature parameter affects acceptance - start with 0.5-1.0. Local minimizer needs bounds for positions [r_min, 1-r_min].

active_paradigms.idea: Use scipy.optimize.dual_annealing for global position optimization with embedded LP radius computation
active_paradigms.description: Dual annealing is a powerful global optimizer combining simulated annealing with local search. Optimize only 2n position variables. The objective function: given positions, compute LP-optimal radii and return negative sum. Bounds for each position: [0.05, 0.95] to allow reasonable radii. Use default parameters with maxiter=2000. The algorithm automatically balances exploration and exploitation. This fundamentally different approach doesn't rely on gradient information and can escape local optima more effectively than SLSQP or trust-constr.
active_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Primary: sum of radii through global position search.
active_paradigms.cautions: Dual annealing is stochastic - use seed for reproducibility. The objective function (LP solve) is called many times - ensure it's efficient. Use bounds to constrain positions. Set appropriate maxiter for timeout constraints. May need restart=True for best results.

tried_paradigms.idea: Use scipy.optimize.minimize with trust-constr for direct constrained optimization of all variables
tried_paradigms.description: Formulate as a constrained optimization problem with 78 variables (52 for centers, 26 for radii). Define objective as negative sum of radii. Use trust-constr method which handles non-linear constraints robustly. Define non-overlap constraints as ||c_i - c_j||^2 >= (r_i + r_j)^2 for all pairs i<j (total 325 constraints). Define boundary constraints: r_i <= c_i[0], r_i <= c_i[1], r_i <= 1-c_i[0], r_i <= 1-c_i[1] (104 constraints). Initialize from hexagonal grid with pre-computed radii. Use multiple random perturbations of initial positions to escape local optima.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: sum_radii through joint optimization of positions and radii
tried_paradigms.cautions: Non-convex problem with many local optima - use at least 10 different initializations. Constraint Jacobian can be computed analytically for efficiency. Set max_iter=1000 and use verbose output to monitor convergence. Some initializations may fail to converge - handle gracefully.

tried_paradigms.idea: Use scipy.spatial.Voronoi for geometric region-based radius maximization
tried_paradigms.description: Compute Voronoi diagram of circle centers using scipy.spatial.Voronoi. For each circle, the maximum possible radius is determined by: (1) half the distance to each Voronoi neighbor, (2) distance to Voronoi cell edges (for interior cells), (3) distance to unit square boundary (for boundary cells). Use Lloyd's algorithm: compute Voronoi diagram, move each center to centroid of its Voronoi cell (clipped to unit square), recompute radii, repeat until convergence. This naturally balances the packing and maximizes minimum distances.
tried_paradigms.cautions: Voronoi cells extending outside the unit square must be clipped. Lloyd's algorithm may not converge for all initial configurations - limit iterations to 100. Centers on boundaries have unbounded Voronoi cells - handle by reflecting points or using bounded Voronoi. The final step should use the radius expansion from the current approach.

tried_paradigms.idea: Use scipy.optimize.differential_evolution with penalty-based constraint handling
tried_paradigms.description: Use differential_evolution for global optimization over all 78 variables. Define bounds: centers in [0.05, 0.95], radii in [0.01, 0.3]. Create objective function = -sum(radii) + penalty where penalty = large_value * sum of constraint violations (overlaps and boundary violations). Use strategy='best1bin', popsize=20, maxiter=500, mutation=(0.5, 1), recombination=0.7. The global search can discover configurations unreachable by local methods. Polish the result with local optimization afterward.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: sum_radii through global exploration of configuration space
tried_paradigms.cautions: Penalty value must be large enough (e.g., 100) to strongly discourage violations but not cause numerical overflow. Set workers=-1 for parallel evaluation if available. The method is slower than local optimization (may take 30-60 seconds) but can find significantly better solutions. Use seed for reproducibility.

tried_paradigms.idea: Use scipy.optimize.minimize with SLSQP for direct constrained optimization
tried_paradigms.description: Formulate the problem as constrained optimization with 78 variables (26 centers × 2 coordinates + 26 radii). Define objective as maximizing sum of radii. Constraints: (1) non-overlap: ||center_i - center_j|| >= r_i + r_j for all pairs, (2) boundary: center.x - r >= 0, center.x + r <= 1, etc. Use SLSQP method which handles nonlinear constraints efficiently. Try multiple random starting points. This directly optimizes the objective rather than iteratively expanding radii.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Primary metric: sum of radii. SLSQP will handle constraints directly rather than the current iterative approach.
tried_paradigms.cautions: SLSQP requires gradient information; use numerical differentiation. Non-overlap constraints are nonlinear (distance formula). Use tight tolerance and multiple starting points. May need 1000+ iterations for convergence.

tried_paradigms.description: Apply differential_evolution with 52 variables (26 circle centers). Bounds: [0,1] for each coordinate. Objective function: compute radii iteratively from positions (using existing compute_radii logic), return negative sum. Differential evolution is population-based and explores broadly, escaping local minima that trap the current local search. Use strategy='best1bin' or 'randtobest1bin', popsize=15-20, mutation=(0.5,1), recombination=0.7. Run with workers=-1 for parallelization.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Primary metric: sum of radii. Global search can find better configurations than local refinement starting from hexagonal grid.
tried_paradigms.cautions: Differential evolution is slower but more thorough. Use larger maxiter (1000+). The objective computes radii from positions, which adds computational cost. Ensure bounds are [small positive, 1-small positive] to avoid boundary issues.

tried_paradigms.idea: Use scipy.optimize.basinhopping with custom local minimizer
tried_paradigms.description: Apply basinhopping which combines local optimization with random jumps to escape local minima. Define 52 variables for positions. Use scipy.optimize.minimize as local minimizer with L-BFGS-B method and bounds. The basin-hopping algorithm perturbs positions randomly and accepts moves based on Metropolis criterion, allowing escape from local minima. Temperature parameter controls acceptance probability. Use niter=100-200, T=0.5-1.0. This hybrid approach balances local refinement with global exploration.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Primary metric: sum of radii. Basin-hopping can escape local minima while still doing efficient local refinement.
tried_paradigms.cautions: Basin-hopping requires tuning temperature parameter. Too high = random search, too low = stuck in local minima. Use take_step callback for custom perturbations. The local minimizer should use bounds to keep positions valid.

tried_paradigms.idea: Use scipy.optimize.linprog for optimal radius computation given fixed positions
tried_paradigms.description: Replace the greedy iterative expand_radii function with linear programming. Given fixed circle positions, finding optimal radii is a linear program: maximize sum(r_i) subject to r_i + r_j <= d_ij (non-overlap) and r_i <= min(x_i, y_i, 1-x_i, 1-y_i) (boundary). Set up the LP with 26 variables, objective vector c=[1,1,...,1], and constraint matrices. Use scipy.optimize.linprog with method='highs' which is fast and robust. This guarantees globally optimal radii for fixed positions, unlike greedy expansion which can get stuck in suboptimal local configurations.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Radii computation - ensures maximum sum of radii for given positions
tried_paradigms.cautions: LP requires careful constraint matrix construction. Non-overlap constraints: for each pair (i,j), add row with +1 at position i and +1 at position j, rhs = distance_ij. Boundary constraints: 4 constraints per circle. Use method='highs' for reliability.

tried_paradigms.idea: Explicit geometric construction with hexagonal lattice pattern optimized for n=26
tried_paradigms.description: Design an explicit arrangement based on a 5-4-5-4-5-3 hexagonal row pattern (26 circles total). Place rows at y positions: r, r+r√3, r+2r√3, etc. Alternate rows with 5 circles (odd rows) and 4 circles (even rows, offset by r). Adjust the final row to place 3 remaining circles. Compute exact positions to maximize corner circle radii (touching two boundaries) and edge circle radii (touching one boundary). Use LP or direct geometric formulas to compute optimal radii. This construction directly encodes known optimal packing structure.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Position design - creates near-optimal starting configuration without search
tried_paradigms.cautions: Hexagonal spacing: horizontal = 2r, vertical = r√3. Adjust r iteratively to fit unit square. Corner circles at (r,r), (1-r,r), (r,1-r), (1-r,1-r) can have larger radii. May need slight adjustments for exact fit.

tried_paradigms.idea: Use scipy.optimize.basinhopping for global position optimization with SLSQP local minimizer
tried_paradigms.description: Replace pure SLSQP with scipy.optimize.basinhopping, which combines random perturbations with local minimization to escape local optima. Configure basinhopping with niter=150-200 iterations, stepsize=0.08-0.12 for perturbations, and use SLSQP as the local minimizer with the same constraint formulation. The random perturbations allow escaping local minima while SLSQP handles constraints locally. After finding good positions, use LP for optimal radius computation. This addresses the local-optimality limitation of pure SLSQP.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Position optimization - global search to find better positions than local SLSQP
tried_paradigms.cautions: Basinhopping doesn't natively handle constraints - use SLSQP as local minimizer which does. Set take_step callback for bounded perturbations. Use accept_test to reject infeasible solutions. May need multiple runs with different seeds.
